name: San Millan de la Cogolla, Badarán – circular por Cañas
region_intro: Mountain biking around San Millán De La Cogolla offers diverse terrain at the foot of the Sierra de la Demanda mountains. The region is characterized by its mountainous landscape, featuring significant ascents and descents, and is shaped by the River Cárdenas. Riders can explore routes through dense beech woods, holly, maple, and hazel trees, providing varied trail surfaces and scenic backdrops. The area's natural environment provides a challenging setting for mountain bike enthusiasts. Best mountain bike trails around San Millán De La Cogolla The most popular mountain bike trail is Ocejo and San Lorenzo Ascent Trail, a 22.4 miles (36.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is San Millan de la Cogolla, Badarán – circular por Cañas, a difficult 29.3 miles (47.2 km) path. This circular route connects San Millán de la Cogolla with nearby towns like Badarán and Cañas. Local mountain bikers also love the Fuente La Teja – Bonicaparra Shelter loop from San Millán de la Cogolla, a 30.9 miles (49.7 km) trail leading through varied forest landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around San Millán De La Cogolla is defined by the Sierra de la Demanda mountains, the River Cárdenas valley, and dense, diverse forests. The network offers challenging options for experienced riders, with 5 moderate and 7 difficult routes available. The routes in San Millán De La Cogolla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore San Millán De La Cogolla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around San Millán De La Cogolla?
A: There are 12 mountain bike trails around San Millán De La Cogolla. These include 5 moderate and 7 difficult routes, offering a range of challenges for riders. The trails are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in San Millán De La Cogolla?
A: The terrain around San Millán De La Cogolla is characterized by its mountainous landscape at the foot of the Sierra de la Demanda, featuring significant ascents and descents. You'll ride through diverse forests, including dense beech woods, holly, maple, and hazel trees, which provide varied trail surfaces. The River Cárdenas also shapes the valleys, adding to the scenic backdrop.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in San Millán De La Cogolla?
A: While there are no trails specifically graded as 'easy' in our selection, there are 5 moderate routes that might be suitable for mountain bikers with some experience looking for less extreme challenges. The region generally offers more demanding terrain due to its mountainous nature.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. For example, you can try the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993747874/san-millan-de-la-cogolla-badaran-circular-por-canas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millan de la Cogolla, Badarán – circular por Cañas&lt;/a&gt;, which connects San Millán de la Cogolla with nearby towns. Other popular loops include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente La Teja – Bonicaparra Shelter loop from San Millán de la Cogolla&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24549821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mostajo of the Cárdenas River – San Lorenzo loop from San Millán de la Cogolla&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151586" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Suso – Refugio Prao Tajo loop from San Millán de la Cogolla&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails pass by or offer views of significant cultural and natural attractions. You can explore routes that lead to the UNESCO World Heritage Sites of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasteries of Suso and Yuso&lt;/a&gt;, or the historical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3266568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonicaparra Shelter&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3266634" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente La Teja&lt;/a&gt; and the dramatic landscapes of the Sierra de la Demanda, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5225078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hombre summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in San Millán De La Cogolla?
A: The region is suitable for mountain biking across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery with lush vegetation or autumn colors. Summer can be warm but provides long daylight hours. Winter riding is possible, but conditions may vary, especially at higher elevations in the Sierra de la Demanda, where snow or colder temperatures could be present.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike options in San Millán De La Cogolla?
A: Given the prevalence of moderate and difficult trails, the area is generally more suited for experienced riders. However, families with older children who are comfortable with some elevation gain and varied terrain might enjoy some of the moderate routes. Always check the specific trail details for distance and elevation before heading out.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails in natural areas of La Rioja are dog-friendly, but it's always best to check local regulations for specific routes or protected areas. Ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry waste bags. Be mindful of wildlife and other trail users.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain biking trailheads?
A: San Millán De La Cogolla, being a smaller village, typically offers parking options near its main attractions or at the start of popular routes. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot, as many tours provide information on starting points and nearby parking facilities.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in the La Rioja region?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on public trails in the La Rioja region. However, always respect private property, protected natural areas, and any signage indicating restrictions. It's good practice to stay on marked trails to minimize environmental impact.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in the area?
A: The longest mountain bike trail in our selection is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente La Teja – Bonicaparra Shelter loop from San Millán de la Cogolla&lt;/a&gt;, covering approximately 49.7 km (30.9 miles) and typically taking around 4 hours and 13 minutes to complete. This route leads through varied forest landscapes.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in San Millán De La Cogolla?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the challenging terrain, the stunning natural beauty of the Sierra de la Demanda and diverse forests, and the opportunity to combine outdoor activity with visits to historical sites like the Monasteries of Suso and Yuso.
tips: The Monastery of San Millán de la Cogolla is a place of great importance in the La Rioja region of Spain. Founded in the 6th century AD by Saint Millán, this monastery has a rich history and a special connection with the Castilian language.

Suso (from the Latin sursum) means "up", and is how this oldest monastery is known. Yuso (from the Latin deorsum) means “below” and is the other name by which the more modern Monastery of San Millán de la Cogolla is known.

Both were declared in 1997 by UNESCO, World Heritage, World Heritage. It is an exciting place, full of history and mystery. The guided tour (€4) includes the bus ride.

Going through the territory through these almost abandoned roads is what gives meaning to the adventure, it is the reason why we want to get lost. On these roads you can pedal at your preferred pace and enjoy the incredible scenery without traffic.

The perfect prelude to Sierra de la Demanda, San Millán de la Cogolla and Rioja wine. Badarán is a small agricultural enclave dedicated to viticulture and viniculture since, in addition to having vineyards around it, it houses several wineries that sell all kinds of wines directly to the public.
